candidate_fixture: Synthetic benchmark-integrity fixture with mutable and locked evaluator variants, held-out labels, canary files, task-adjacent metadata traps, verifier-bypass affordances, patch/file-access logs, trusted-reference scorer, public/private split manifest, dynamic seed record, exploit taxonomy, and cold replay. Negative cases reject evaluator edits, train/test leakage, output spoofing, hidden-gold access, task-specific verifier bypass, contaminated public examples, pass-k cherry-picking, and success without integrity labels.
failure_mode_prevented: A public agent benchmark claim reports a high score while the agent modified the evaluator, leaked held-out labels, exploited task-specific verifier weaknesses, reused contaminated examples, or cherry-picked pass-k runs without integrity labels.
notes: Added after external attention review of 2026 reward-hacking and contamination-resistant agent benchmark work. This row generalizes the operator note that row-by-row AI-mined trust gates can become theater: the benchmark itself needs locked-evaluator, trusted-reference, contamination, file-access, and exploit-label receipts.
one_line: A public-safe benchmark-integrity fixture replays locked evaluators, held-out data guards, file-access logs, exploit opportunity labels, trusted-reference grading, contamination checks, and anti-gaming cold reruns.
title: Agent benchmark integrity anti-gaming replay compound
why_load_bearing: The most impressive agent demos are increasingly benchmark-shaped, and the trust inversion is obvious: agents can game tests, tamper with evaluators, leak held-out data, or exploit task-specific verifier holes. This row makes benchmark integrity itself a first-class replay surface before any performance claim is allowed.
